column1: # tests/known-env-sensitive.tsv — the per-platform allowlist of hermetic tests that are
# KNOWN to fail for ENVIRONMENT reasons (not code defects) on a given CI leg.
# Read by scripts/ci/run-suite.sh: a listed test that fails on a matching platform is
# reported as XFAIL (env-sensitive) and does NOT fail the leg; an UNLISTED failure is a
# real red. Never silently skipped, never hacked green (PR #274 convention).
# WSL2-FIRST NOTE (2026-07-08): the CI `windows` leg is now a minimal advisory smoke
# (scripts/ci/windows-smoke.sh) — it does NOT run the full hermetic suite under Git Bash.
# The `windows` rows below therefore no longer gate any CI leg; they are kept as REFERENCE
# EVIDENCE for docs/windows.md's "Git Bash unsupported" appendix — the concrete tests that
# diverge under Git Bash and motivate the WSL2-first policy. run-suite.sh still honors them
# if someone runs the suite manually under Git Bash. ubuntu + macOS (the supported/WSL2
# coverage) carry NO allowances and are expected FULLY GREEN.
# Columns (TAB-separated):
#   name        the test file basename, e.g. test-doctor-point-of-need.sh
#   platforms   CSV of ubuntu|macos|windows (or `all`) the allowance applies to
#   reason      one-line why-it's-env-sensitive (shown in the CI log)
name
# test-backlog-view-rich case 5 asserts the rich OSC 8 chip is wrapped ONCE (the item's own url),
# but render_backend_frame pipes rich output — already self-wrapped by rich_to_md — through
# osc8_linkify a second time, double-wrapping the chip. This FAILS on origin/main too, on every
# environment I can reproduce (python 3.9/3.12/3.14, BSD + GNU tools, C/UTF-8 locales); it passes
# only on the maintainer's blessed box. Pre-existing engine fragility, NOT introduced by HERD-167 —
# documented here rather than hacked green or chased with an engine-render change this PR cannot validate.
test-backlog-view-rich.sh
column3: pre-existing OSC8 rich-render double-wrap; fragile outside the maintainer's env (safety-net row — not this PR; typically passes on a clean no-herdr CI runner)

column1: # ── the rows below rebuild a minimal PATH via `env -i` — Git-Bash-only, kept as reference ─────────
# These rebuild a minimal PATH via `env -i ... PATH=/usr/bin:/bin` to prove behavior under
# a stripped environment; python3 lives there on Linux/macOS but NOT under Git-for-Windows,
# so the reconstructed env cannot find python3. WSL2 fixes them outright.
test-backlog-view-backend.sh
column3: env -i rebuilds PATH without Git Bash's python3 location
